primary_signals.summary: Stop-texting performative boundary manufacture — Melissa says 'stop texting me' then immediately sends 7+ more messages; when Eli responds to her new texts she repeats 'stop texting me.' Classic ADD-006 manufactured boundary-violation cycle creating false court evidence of Eli violating her requests. Sev-2: deliberate evidence manufacturing. ARC-RF12-002 promotes CANDIDATE to WATCHING.

'For the record' — explicit court-documentation language in message text. This is a direct instance of Melissa naming her documentation intent in the message itself (ADD-005 partial analog — explicit documentation language per ADD-002 exception). ARC-RF12-001 promotes CANDIDATE to WATCHING. Note: Sev-1 (documentation language present but not the full explicit 'documenting your responses for my court case' admission — that statement is confirmed by Eli at CP2 but not yet keyword-located as its own message).

Stop-contacting + harassment threat then 9+ messages — Melissa demands Eli stop contacting her, threatens harassment claim, then immediately sends 9+ messages. Intensified ADD-006 pattern with RF-05 legal threat (harassment) added as escalation mechanism. Sev-2: manufactured harassment pretext + immediate continuation.
compound: RF-07 compound (manufactured flooding per RF-07 XREF)

primary_signals.summary: 'My lawyer already said I had a strong case this further demonstrates that' — explicit documentation-for-court framing tied to a specific legal strategy. Melissa acknowledges the exchange is being collected as legal evidence in real time. Sev-2: active court-record framing within the message itself.

Post-police-report probe tone shift (Nov 22 2025 anchor) — Melissa shifts to warm, concerned, information-seeking register after Eli's first police report. CP2-confirmed ADD-004 pattern: 'What's going on with you? You seem different. If you want to talk' — intelligence-gathering disguised as care. Legal correlation: high (within 30 days of police report Nov 22 2025). ARC-RF12-001 promotes WATCHING to ACTIVE. NOTE: Specific message not keyword-located — signal confirmed by CP2 Q2(a)/(b); manual verification recommended in B-030 area.

ARC-RF12-001.name: Strategic Campaign
ARC-RF12-001.note: Three signals: 'For the record' documentation language (S-002), lawyer-case framing (S-004), post-police-report tone probe (S-005). Explicit documentation admission confirmed by Eli at CP2 ('She has said she mentioned she is documenting my responses for her court case') — the specific message containing this statement was not keyword-located in B-001 through B-031; if found, classify as RF-12 primary Sev-2 additional signal. Jun 2022 contempt summons window (B-004/B-005) was identified as a legal-event anchor to check (ADD-001) but no tone-shift signal documented there — contempt summons is RF-06 primary domain. GAL appointment April 2026 (B-031 area) is the final legal event anchor — S-005's police-report arc likely continues through this window.

ARC-RF12-002.name: Bait-and-Switch Cycle
ARC-RF12-002.note: Two confirmed signals (S-001 B-020, S-003 B-025) — both are stop-texting manufactured boundary violations (ADD-006). Never promoted to ACTIVE (requires clearer bait-switch cycle sequence or additional stop-texting pattern). Bait-switch cycle per ADD-007 (reasonable entry → de-escalation by Eli → Melissa escalates) is CP2-confirmed as the structural dynamic but no discrete bait-switch sequence was keyword-identifiable as a standalone arc signal separate from the RF-04 DARVO compounds. ARC-RF12-002 closes at WATCHING — the bait-switch cycle is a modality confirmed by CP2 and evidenced through RF-04 compounds rather than independent RF-12 signals.

bait_switch_index.note: Per RF-12-SUPPLEMENT.md: generate bait-switch-index.json tracking each identified bait-and-switch sequence. Deferred to Phase 2 pattern synthesis — the index requires cross-pass RF-04/RF-09 review to identify which compounds were RF-12 bait-switch constructions. The index should be generated during RC-PATTERN-SYNTHESIS.md execution, not as a standalone RF-12 batch output.

eli_phase_b_c_note.classification_applied: Eli's Phase B/C communication evolution was NOT flagged as RF-12 throughout this pass. His AI-guided communication (EP-1078, May 2025 onward) is genuine improvement — not court-record management. Only flagged Eli if explicit documentation language or performative baiting present (none found).

methodology_notes: Low keyword yield correctly anticipated: most RF-12 signals are structural (tone shifts, absence of escalation, probe patterns) rather than keyword-identifiable. The 5 primary signals represent the clearest keyword-anchored or CP2-confirmed instances.
ADD-006 stop-texting pattern produced 2 of 5 signals (S-001, S-003) — this is the most readily keyword-identifiable RF-12 sub-type.

ADD-005 explicit documentation language produced 2 signals (S-002 'for the record'; S-004 'my lawyer already said I had a strong case') — the full explicit documentation admission Eli described at CP2 ('She has said she mentioned she is documenting my responses for her court case') was not located as a discrete message. If found in manual review, adds a Sev-2 signal.
ADD-004 police-report tone shift (S-005) is the only CP2-confirmed-but-not-keyword-located signal. Manual verification recommended before Phase 3 evidence compilation.

ADD-001 legal timeline applied to all signals: Jun 2022 summons (B-004/B-005) produced no RF-12 tone-shift signals; Nov 22 2025 police report (B-030) produced S-005; Apr 2026 GAL appointment window is in dataset close period.

RF-04 DARVO compounds are candidates for RF-12 bait-switch peripheral notes per ADD-008 projection pattern rule — those compounds should be reviewed during Phase 2 synthesis for strategic accusation timing.
